step_by_step_thinking:
I concur with the initial assessment that Option B ("Rake angle") correctly identifies the angle formed between the rake face and radial line. This alignment stems from standard definitions in metalworking where the "rake angle" specifically denotes the measurement taken between these two components - the rake face itself and the radial plane extending through the tool’s axis. Such an understanding not only facilitates proper interpretation but also supports effective implementation across various manufacturing processes requiring meticulous control over tool geometry parameters. Therefore, my agreement reinforces confidence in selecting Option B as the accurate response among available choices. 

Answer: <B>
